annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58006,"title":"[MySql] Docs: Add examples and complete README for Azure.Provisioning.MySql initial beta release","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57985) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed The initial beta release (`1.0.0-beta.1`) of `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` was published on 2026-04-09. The package provides declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, exposing resource types including `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, and more. ## Gaps Found - **Examples section is completely empty** — no code samples showing how to create a `MySqlFlexibleServer` or any child resources (databases, firewall rules, etc.) - **Authenticate the Client section** has the heading but no content — should explain that deployment is done via `azd` and point to provisioning docs - **Next steps section is empty** — no links to related packages, REST API docs, or Azure portal - **Key concepts section lacks resource descriptions** — lists no individual resource types or their purpose; the library exposes 11+ provisionable resource classes with no guidance on which to use - **No sample test files exist** — `tests/` contains only a `.csproj`; there are no `Samples/` files with snippet-backed examples that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool can inject into the README 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` Fill in the empty/incomplete sections: **Authenticate the Client section** — replace the empty section with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client This library uses the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) to deploy the declarative infrastructure you define. There is no runtime client authentication required — provisioning resources is done via `azd up` or similar commands. For more information see the [Azure Developer CLI documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` **Key concepts section** — replace the current one-liner with: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates. Key resource types provided: - **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — The primary resource representing an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server. Configure SKU, version, storage, backup, high availability, and network settings. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — A database hosted on a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — A firewall rule controlling network access to a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — A server-level configuration parameter. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — An Azure AD administrator account for a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** — Represents a server backup. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Scheduled maintenance window configuration. - **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Threat protection settings for a Flexible Server. ``` **Examples section** — replace the empty section with a snippet placeholder: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server with a Database and Firewall Rule ``` ``` ``` **Next steps section** — replace the empty section with: ```markdown ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Learn about the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Explore related provisioning packages: [`Azure.Provisioning`]((www.nuget.org/redacted), [`Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault`]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [Category("Samples")] public void CreateBasicMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic Infrastructure infra = new(); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new("mysqlServer", MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { AdministratorLogin = "mysqladmin", Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ads_v5", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled }, HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability { Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled } }; infra.Add(server); MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new("mysqlDb") { Parent = server, Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infra.Add(database); MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new("allowAzureServices") { Parent = server, StartIPAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIPAddress = "0.0.0.0" }; infra.Add(firewallRule); #endregion } } } ``` **Snippet details:** - Snippet name: `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` - Test file path: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` - README location: under `## Examples`, the placeholder `` ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic `` followed by closing `` ``` `` ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current state of `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Heading only, no content | | Key concepts | ⚠️ One-liner, no resource type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ Empty | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for canonical formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects the `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` snippet into `README.md`; verify the git diff shows the snippet was inserted correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — only if any public types or members were added 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24201264947/agentic_workflow) · ● 532.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58006","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T16:39:10Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T16:39:10Z"}

Configure SKU, version, storage, backup, high availability, and network settings. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — A database hosted on a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — A firewall rule controlling network access to a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — A server-level configuration parameter. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — An Azure AD administrator account for a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** — Represents a server backup. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Scheduled maintenance window configuration. - **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Threat protection settings for a Flexible Server. ``` **Examples section** — replace the empty section with a snippet placeholder: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server with a Database and Firewall Rule ``` ``` ``` **Next steps section** — replace the empty section with: ```markdown ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Learn about the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Explore related provisioning packages: [`Azure.Provisioning`]((www.nuget.org/redacted), [`Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault`]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [Category("Samples")] public void CreateBasicMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic Infrastructure infra = new(); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new("mysqlServer", MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { AdministratorLogin = "mysqladmin", Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ads_v5", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled }, HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability { Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled } }; infra.Add(server); MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new("mysqlDb") { Parent = server, Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infra.Add(database); MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new("allowAzureServices") { Parent = server, StartIPAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIPAddress = "0.0.0.0" }; infra.Add(firewallRule); #endregion } } } ``` **Snippet details:** - Snippet name: `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` - Test file path: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` - README location: under `## Examples`, the placeholder `` ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic `` followed by closing `` ``` `` ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current state of `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Heading only, no content | | Key concepts | ⚠️ One-liner, no resource type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ Empty | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for canonical formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects the `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` snippet into `README.md`; {"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3292095671,"number":51705,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"CosmosDBAccountCapability to implement IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>","body":"## Background
This is a "sub-issue" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).

## Request
It would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>`. It would make the "upserting" of CosmosDB capabilities within the Aspire framework much easier if it were simple to compare 2 `CosmosDBAccountCapability` for equality based on their `Name` property alone.","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"goodcoffeecode","id":1698190,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTgxOTA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1698190?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/goodcoffeecode","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz"},{"id":1369687945,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question","name":"question","color":"eaa875","description":"The issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved. Most issues start as arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue requests completing the README for the newly released `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` 1.0.0-beta.1 package, including filling in empty Examples, Authenticate the Client, Key concepts, and Next steps sections, plus adding a sample test file with snippet-backed code.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` 1.0.0-beta.1
**Affected API:** Documentation / README for `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, and related resource types
**Scenarios:**
- README `## Examples` section is empty — no code samples for creating a MySQL Flexible Server or child resources
- `## Authenticate the Client` has a heading but no content
- `## Next steps` section is empty — no links to related packages or documentation
- `## Key concepts` section lacks descriptions of the 11+ provisionable resource classes
- No `Samples/` test files exist for snippet injection via `Update-Snippets.ps1`

**Root ask:** Implement a complete README with working code snippets and a sample test file for the `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` initial beta release.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This issue was automatically generated by the Update Docs workflow (run 24201264947), triggered by commit [REDACTED] (PR #57985). It is a documentation gap report, not a runtime defect.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` to confirm the empty/incomplete sections listed in the issue
2. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as a canonical formatting reference
3. Create `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` with a `#region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` block and add the C# snippet placeholder to the README
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` to inject the snippet, then verify via `git diff`
5. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to validate the new sample compiles and passes
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Provisioning` — the package name `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` starts with `Azure.Provisioning`, matching the Provisioning category definition
- **Service:** `MySQL` — the package targets Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server; `%MySQL` is the matching service label in CODEOWNERS (line 776)
- **Confidence:** High — the package name unambiguously identifies both the category and service; no other category or service labels were plausible
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %MySQL` (line 776) — the predicted "MySQL" service label directly matches this entry's single label requirement
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ajlam @ambhatna @kummanish
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; no assignment (ServiceOwners only path); no "needs-team-attention" (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported); @mentioned ServiceOwners via routing comment
- **Scan notes:** Scanned from end of file (~line 1230) upward; all management-specific entries (lines 900–1230) require `%Mgmt` or management service labels — no match; `%Provisioning` catch-all at line 415 was not reached since `%MySQL` at line 776 matched first
</details>
